title: AVA Commit Log Entry
description: One JSONL record per retired instruction, emitted in strict program order by both RTL harness (Agent B) and ISS golden backend (Agent C). Consumer: Agent D (comparator), Agent F (coverage director), red-team agents. Schema version 2.0.0.

schema_version.description: Commit-log schema version. Must match the $id minor.patch. Producers MUST emit this; consumers MUST reject records whose version does not match the version they were compiled against.

hex8.description: Exactly 8 lowercase hex digits with 0x prefix — represents a 32-bit value.

hex4.description: Exactly 4 lowercase hex digits with 0x prefix — represents a 16-bit value.

hex2.description: Exactly 2 lowercase hex digits with 0x prefix — represents an 8-bit value.

hex_word_or_half_or_byte.description: A hex value of exactly 2, 4, or 8 hex digits (byte, half, word). Width must match the `size` field of its parent mem entry.

reg_name.description: Integer register name x1..x31. x0 is FORBIDDEN in any delta — its value is always 0 by the RISC-V specification and must never be written.

csr_name.description: Canonical lowercase CSR name. The permitted set is the complete M-mode + debug CSR space for RV32IM. Extend this enum (and bump schema version) when adding S/U/H/V modes.

trap_object.description: Synchronous exception or asynchronous interrupt taken at this commit boundary.

cause.description: mcause written by hardware: bit[31]=interrupt, bits[30:0]=exception code.

tval.description: mtval written by hardware. 0x00000000 for exceptions where tval is not defined.

tvec.description: mtvec at the moment the trap is taken (BASE field, mode stripped). The actual handler PC may differ for vectored mode.

epc.description: mepc written by hardware — the PC of the trapping instruction or the next instruction for interrupts.

is_interrupt.description: Convenience flag: true iff cause[31]=1. Producers MUST set this consistently with `cause`; comparator checks both.

exception_code.description: Numeric value of cause[30:0]. Provided as an integer for human readability. Must equal parseInt(cause,16) & 0x7FFFFFFF.

mem_write_entry.description: One naturally-aligned store or AMO write event. For unaligned stores that become two bus transactions, emit two entries in address order.

addr.description: Physical (= M-mode virtual for bare-metal) byte address of the start of the write.

size.description: Width of write in bytes. SB=1, SH=2, SW=4.

data.description: Data written. Width (in hex chars) MUST match size: size=1 → 2 hex chars (0xNN), size=2 → 4 hex chars (0xNNNN), size=4 → 8 hex chars (0xNNNNNNNN).

amo_op.description: Optional: AMO operation type. Absent for plain stores. If present, `data` is the *result value written back* to memory (post-operation).

mem_read_entry.description: One load / AMO read event. Optional — emit only when the coverage director or comparator is configured with `--log-loads`. Default is stores-only.

hart.description: Hart ID (0-based). Single-core runs always emit 0. Multi-hart runs (Phase 2) interleave records; Agent D must sort by (hart, seq) before per-hart comparison.

seq.description: Monotonically increasing retirement sequence number for this hart, starting at 0. Provides a total order for comparison and detects log truncation. Must increment by exactly 1 per record; Agent D raises SEQ_GAP if a gap is found.

cycle.description: Wall-clock simulation cycle at commit (RTL only). ISS backends MUST emit null. Comparator IGNORES this field during diff — it is present for timing analysis only.

pc.description: Program counter of the committed instruction. For compressed (RVC) instructions this is the address of the 16-bit parcel. Must be 2-byte aligned; Agent D raises ALIGNMENT_ERROR for odd PCs.

instr.description: Raw instruction encoding. Full 32-bit instructions: 8 hex digits (0xNNNNNNNN). Compressed 16-bit: 4 hex digits (0xNNNN). Width must match instr_width.

instr_width.description: Instruction width in bytes: 4 for standard 32-bit instructions (RV32IM baseline), 2 for future RVC. Must be consistent with the `instr` hex digit count.

disasm.description: Optional human-readable disassembly string, e.g. 'mul a0,a0,a1'. Ignored by the comparator. Useful for waveform annotation and bug reports.

priv.description: Privilege mode at commit. 'M'=Machine, 'S'=Supervisor, 'U'=User, 'D'=Debug. RV32IM M-mode only for schema v2; emit 'M' always. Enum kept open for forward compatibility.

trap.description: Non-null when a trap is taken at this retirement boundary. Null (or omitted) otherwise. NOTE: If trap is non-null, the instruction's architectural side-effects may still be committed (e.g. `ecall` retires before trap). Producers must document their behaviour; see interfaces.md §6.

regs.description: Register file delta: only registers whose value changed as a direct result of this instruction's execution. x0 is FORBIDDEN — its value is architecturally fixed at 0x00000000. An empty object {} is valid and expected for stores, branches (not taken), fences, NOPs, and ecalls.
regs.description_note: RV32IM instructions write at most 1 register. maxProperties=1 catches multi-write bugs in harness code.

csrs.description: CSR delta: only CSRs whose value changed at this commit. Empty object {} is valid. Keys must be canonical lowercase names from the approved enum. Values are 32-bit hex.

oneOf.description: One entry for aligned stores; two entries for unaligned stores that cross a word boundary. AMO operations emit one write entry (the store half).

mem_writes.description: Present and non-null for store / AMO instructions. Null or absent for all other instruction classes.

mem_reads.description: Optional load-side logging. Disabled by default. Enable with run_manifest.dut.log_loads=true. When enabled, absent is equivalent to null.

fp_regs.description: Floating-point register delta. Reserved for RV32F/D extension (not in scope for v2). Producers targeting RV32IM MUST omit or set to null. Schema will be extended in v3.

_src.description: Producer tag. 'rtl' = Verilator/VCS harness; 'iss' = Spike; 'sail' = Sail-RISC-V; 'manual' = hand-crafted test fixture. Comparator strips this field before hashing for equality. Never use this field for routing logic.

_run_id.description: Run ID from the run manifest. Injected by the producer to allow log records to be self-identifying when concatenated across runs. Must match manifest.run_id.

examples._comment: Example 1: MUL a0, a0, a1  (RV32M multiply)
Example 3: DIV a0, a0, zero  (divide-by-zero → result = -1 = 0xFFFFFFFF, no trap per spec)
Example 4: ECALL  (M-mode environment call → trap cause=11)
Example 5: SH a0, 0(sp)  (16-bit store — validates hex4 data)
